top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Trek 100's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Trek 100's rolling hills rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Trek 100's rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Trek 100. Train it until it feels boring.
At Trek 100, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
When someone attacks on rolling hills, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,500ft of climbing on rolling hills does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 100 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Trek 100, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: June in Trek HQ, 801 West Madison Street, Waterloo, Wisconsin 53594 can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Trek 100. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Trek 100’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Trek 100 requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling hills to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 100 miles of rolling hills in Trek HQ, 801 West Madison Street, Waterloo, Wisconsin 53594.
